subject: [PMSH] Finalise API
commit_message:
[PMSH] Finalise API

Finalised API for release J

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-3061

Signed-off-by: egernug <gerard.nugent@est.tech>
Change-Id: I54c13d4d1a1b5576b963f94b690b70d913aef4b8

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/rtdv3-global-verify-master/5863/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/rtdv3-global-verify-master/5863

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-master-verify-java/865/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-master-verify-java/865

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

It's my understanding that the /v1 paths should not be removed

and that all new paths should be available under both the /v2 level in addition to their being available at the top level.

Is there a reason that these updates were not done that way?

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> It's my understanding that the /v1 paths should not be removed
> 
> and that all new paths should be available under both the /v2 level in addition to their being available at the top level.
> 
> Is there a reason that these updates were not done that way?

I am looking for some confirmation on this. The only api call being continued in the new version is the healthcheck and that is unchanged. Therefore, I don't believe that this needs versioning.
All other api calls are new calls being implemented for the first time in this release.
As I say I'm trying to find some clear documentation on this case. I'm open to any suggestions.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/rtdv3-global-verify-master/5872/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/rtdv3-global-verify-master/5872

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-master-verify-java/866/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-master-verify-java/866

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> It's my understanding that the /v1 paths should not be removed
> 
> and that all new paths should be available under both the /v2 level in addition to their being available at the top level.
> 
> Is there a reason that these updates were not done that way?

After talking to the team it would appear that no API has ever been published in the PMSH documentation before. As such we don't believe that we need to version here as this is the first official API that will be published and documented for the PMSH product. We can prefix v1 in the URI if necessary but don't believe any other action should be needed. Is this an acceptable approach? We are hoping to have this resolved and merged for M2.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> > 
> > It's my understanding that the /v1 paths should not be removed
> > 
> > and that all new paths should be available under both the /v2 level in addition to their being available at the top level.
> > 
> > Is there a reason that these updates were not done that way?
> 
> After talking to the team it would appear that no API has ever been published in the PMSH documentation before. As such we don't believe that we need to version here as this is the first official API that will be published and documented for the PMSH product. We can prefix v1 in the URI if necessary but don't believe any other action should be needed. Is this an acceptable approach? We are hoping to have this resolved and merged for M2.

This seems like breaking change however if old basepath & v1 apis were never used externally, fine with removing it. 
And / is valid basepath under swagger 2.0 spec - so change seems okay.
Change has been successfully merged by Tony Hansen
